{
  "name": "neuman-brain-v2",
  "private": true,
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "description": "Neuman Brain - Ultimate Management Tool (React + TypeScript rebuild)",
  "author": "Travis Neuman",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc -b && vite build",
    "build:production": "NODE_ENV=production npm run build",
    "lint": "eslint . --config config/eslint.config.js",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "test": "vitest --config config/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:ui": "vitest --ui --config config/vitest.config.ts",
    "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage --config config/vitest.config.ts",
    "type-check": "tsc -b --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.69.0",
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^10.0.0",
    "@dnd-kit/utilities": "^3.2.2",
    "@google/generative-ai": "^0.24.1",
    "@huggingface/inference": "^4.13.3",
    "@lexical/code": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/link": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/list": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/markdown": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/react": "^0.38.2",
    "@lexical/rich-text": "^0.38.2",
    "@mistralai/mistralai": "^1.10.0",
    "@tanstack/react-query": "^5.90.7",
    "@types/crypto-js": "^4.2.2",
    "crypto-js": "^4.2.0",
    "date-fns": "^4.1.0",
    "dexie": "^4.2.1",
    "framer-motion": "^12.23.24",
    "groq-sdk": "^0.35.0",
    "ics": "^3.8.1",
    "leaflet": "^1.9.4",
    "lexical": "^0.38.2",
    "openai": "^6.9.0",
    "react": "^19.2.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.0",
    "react-grid-layout": "^1.5.2",
    "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
    "react-masonry-css": "^1.0.16",
    "react-router-dom": "^7.9.5",
    "uuid": "^13.0.0",
    "zustand": "^5.0.8"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.1",
    "@testing-library/jest-dom": "^6.9.1",
    "@testing-library/react": "^16.3.0",
    "@testing-library/user-event": "^14.6.1",
    "@types/leaflet": "^1.9.21",
    "@types/node": "^24.10.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.2.2",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.2.2",
    "@types/react-grid-layout": "^1.3.5",
    "@types/uuid": "^10.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^5.1.0",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.21",
    "eslint": "^9.39.1",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "^5.2.0",
    "eslint-plugin-react-refresh": "^0.4.24",
    "globals": "^16.5.0",
    "jsdom": "^27.1.0",
    "postcss": "^8.5.6",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.4.18",
    "typescript": "~5.9.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.46.3",
    "vite": "^7.2.2",
    "vitest": "^4.0.8"
  }
}

content.content:
1→import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
     2→import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
     3→
     4→// https://vite.dev/config/
     5→export default defineConfig({
     6→  plugins: [react()],
     7→
     8→  // CSS configuration with PostCSS
     9→  css: {
    10→    postcss: './config/postcss.config.js',
    11→  },
    12→
    13→  // Base public path - use '/' for root domain deployment
    14→  base: '/',
    15→
    16→  // Build optimizations
    17→  build: {
    18→    // Output directory
    19→    outDir: 'dist',
    20→
    21→    // Generate sourcemaps for production debugging (optional)
    22→    sourcemap: false,
    23→
    24→    // Chunk size warnings
    25→    chunkSizeWarningLimit: 1000,
    26→
    27→    // Rollup options - Simplified vendor splitting (no separate react chunk)
    28→    rollupOptions: {
    29→      output: {
    30→        // Split heavy libraries that are lazy-loaded into separate chunks
    31→        // Keep React in main vendor chunk to ensure correct load order
    32→        manualChunks(id) {
    33→          if (id.includes('node_modules')) {
    34→            // Heavy libraries that are lazy-loaded can be split
    35→            if (id.includes('dnd-kit')) {
    36→              return 'dnd-vendor';
    37→            }
    38→            if (id.includes('lexical')) {
    39→              return 'lexical-vendor';
    40→            }
    41→            // Everything else (including React, react-router, leaflet) stays in vendor
    42→            // This ensures React loads before any code that depends on it
    43→            return 'vendor';
    44→          }
    45→        },
    46→      },
    47→    },
    48→  },
    49→
    50→  // Development server
    51→  server: {
    52→    port: 5173,
    53→    strictPort: false,
    54→    host: true,
    55→  },
    56→
    57→  // Preview server (for testing production build)
    58→  preview: {
    59→    port: 4173,
    60→    strictPort: false,
    61→    host: true,
    62→  },
    63→})
    64→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
